description: Design the budget check-and-deduct operation for a real-time bidding platform. Campaigns have daily budgets stored in Redis. When a bid opportunity arrives, the system must check if remaining budget ≥ bid amount and, if so, atomically deduct it. The system processes 80,000 bid requests per second across 12 pods. Design the budget check-and-deduct that prevents overspend under concurrent load.
context: Current broken implementation: GET budget; if budget >= bid: DECRBY budget bid. This has a TOCTOU race: two pods can both read budget=100 and both deduct 80, resulting in -60 overspend. The fix must be atomic. Redis version: 7.2. Kafka topic 'financial-events' is available for audit. Each bid deduction must be traceable — write an audit event to Kafka after every successful deduction.

checks.text: Does the proposal use a single atomic Redis operation (Lua script, MULTI/EXEC, or similar) for the check-and-deduct — not a separate GET followed by a conditional DECRBY?
Does the proposal explicitly explain why non-atomic check-then-deduct fails under concurrent load (race condition / TOCTOU)?
Does the proposal publish a bid deduction event to Kafka (financial-events or similar audit topic) after each successful deduction?

should_prune.description: Proposal that reads budget with GET then conditionally decrements with DECRBY as two separate commands
should_prune.violates: CONSTRAINT-004 — budget check-and-deduct must be atomic; two-command pattern has TOCTOU race

should_prune.description: Proposal that uses Redis WATCH/MULTI/EXEC optimistic lock but retries indefinitely under high contention without a circuit breaker
should_prune.violates: CONSTRAINT-004 — under 80K TPS, unbounded retry loops cause cascading latency spikes

should_pass: Proposal using Redis Lua script: atomically checks remaining budget >= bid_amount, decrements if true, returns new balance. After successful Lua deduction, publishes {campaign_id, bid_amount, timestamp, request_id} to Kafka financial-events topic. Idempotency key prevents double-deduction on retry.
